texto: I believe that our lives, just like fairy tales - the stories that have been written by us humans, through our own experiences of living - will always have a Hero and a Heroine, a Fairy Godmother and a Wicked Witch.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lucinda Riley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “I believe that our lives, just like fairy tales - the stories that have been written by us humans…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, fairy-tales, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: I believe that our lives, just like fairy tales - the stories that have been written by us humans, through our own experiences of living - will always have a Hero and a Heroine, a Fairy Godmother and a Wicked Witch.

texto: I felt the protective dam I'd built around my heart began to crumble. I turned my eyes back to him and felt the love finally start to trickle out through the fissures. And hoped that one day it would become a torrent.
explicacao: Citação de Lucinda Riley sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “I felt the protective dam I'd built around my heart began to crumble. I turned my eyes back to hi…” articula amor, love-quotes, reflexao em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: I felt the protective dam I'd built around my heart began to crumble. I turned my eyes back to him and felt the love finally start to trickle out through the fissures. And hoped that one day it would become a torrent.
